description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of four Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel samples, designated 3Cr, 2Ni, 2Ni3Cr, and 2Ni5Cr, all subjected to the same hot rolling and ultra-fast cooling processing. The x-axis records 2θ angles ranging from 30° to 100°, while the y-axis denotes diffraction intensity in arbitrary units (a.u.). All four samples display identical characteristic diffraction peaks assigned to the face-centred cubic (FCC) γ austenite phase, indexed as γ(111), γ(200), γ(220), γ(311), and γ(222). No additional peaks corresponding to secondary phases (such as B2 phase with body-centred cubic structure) are detected, proving all tested steels possess a single γ austenite phase structure. This XRD result is consistent with EBSD analysis findings, validating that the studied Ni and Cr alloying strategy does not introduce unwanted intermetallic secondary phases in this low-density steel system.
